survivors_flagged.one_line: Novonor control sale to Shine I closed Jun-2026; buyer must register a Brazil mandatory tender offer for minorities at control terms — pending dated MTO is a live arb for ADR holders.
Definitive Feb-2026 deal to merge with Pasqal (quantum) at $2B, close/Nasdaq listing targeted ~Jul-1-2026; trust-floor downside + de-SPAC optionality, but quantum-hype quality is thin.
Multiple hard dated catalysts: BBP-418 PDUFA Nov 27 2026, encaleret NDA 1H26, infigratinib NDA Q3 2026, plus Attruby ramp.
Definitive merger w/ Exascale Labs (Jan 2026), S-4/A on file; SPAC trades ~$10.39 near trust — dated close but thin asymmetry/redemption risk.
Live merger-arb: Aquarian $70/sh all-cash, shareholder vote PASSED Feb 12 2026, closing 2026 pending insurance-reg approval; ~10% gross spread at $63.5 = real dated, mispriced-asymmetry special-sit.
HoldCo Asset Mgmt building stake ($35.8M) pushing 'strategic alternatives'; 2026 annual meeting / potential proxy battle is a forward hardening trigger, but no formal sale process yet.
Live $80/sh unsolicited Clearlake (18.9% holder) take-private proposal under board review; poison pill repealed — hardening merger-arb.
Jana activist + board exploring sale + Outback Brazil divestiture in active process; structural step pending.
Tinlarebant NDA completed Q2-26 under BTD; 60-day acceptance then PDUFA date — dated regulatory binary in Stargardt.
Formal sale process with Morgan Stanley (Jun-26) at record-low shares; live take-private/strategic-alternatives review.

verified.catalyst: After Shine I FIP closed its 50.11%-voting Braskem control purchase from Novonor/NSP (Jun-2026), the buyer must run a change-of-control mandatory tender offer (OPA, Art. 254-A) for 100% of ON+PN shares at the same per-share terms paid to NSP — but the consideration is NSP debentures, not cash.
verified.dated_milestone: OPA registration request filed with CVM and B3 on 2026-06-11; next: CVM/B3 approval then OPA launch/auction (no firm date set, "as soon as possible" per filing)
verified.bloom_gates: G1 (named counterparty Shine I FIP / Vórtx-IG4) + G2 (concrete commitment: OPA filed with CVM/B3 2026-06-11) PASS; G3 FAILS — no cash per-share figure; consideration is illiquid NSP (in judicial recovery) debentures, and BRKM5 already re-rated +11% on the Jun-11 announcement
verified.edge: L — the "arb" pays distressed-issuer (NSP, recuperação judicial) debentures rather than a cash control-terms floor, so there is no clean spread to capture; the catalyst has also largely fired (+11% on announce), and OPA launch is gated on undated CVM/B3 approval
verified.primary_source: Braskem 6-K / B3 fato relevante via StockTitan + Brazilian press (Money Times, Suno, Ativo Virtual), 2026-06-11; CADE clearance + closing 6-Ks Jun-2026
verified.analysis: The merger-arb is real and dated: Shine I FIP (Vortx/IG4) closed the 50.11%-voting Braskem control buy from Novonor/NSP in Jun-2026 and filed the mandatory change-of-control OPA with CVM and B3 on 2026-06-11 for 100% of common and preferred shares at the same terms paid to NSP. The fatal flaw versus a classic arb is that "control terms" = NSP debentures (2x 1st-series + 1x 2nd-series of NSP's 2nd issuance), not cash, and NSP is in judicial recovery — so minorities/ADR holders face an illiquid, hard-to-value distressed payout, not a cash floor, while the equity already jumped ~11% on the Jun-11 announcement. Real + dated + forward (OPA launch pending CVM/B3 approval), but no specific unpriced cash figure and weak/already-rerated edge keep it WATCH, not ACTIVE.

verified.catalyst: Bleichroeder Acquisition Corp II's definitive $2.0B business combination with neutral-atom quantum firm Pasqal closes and re-lists on Nasdaq as "Pasqal," with a ~$10.08 trust floor protecting downside ahead of the vote.
verified.dated_milestone: SPAC close + Nasdaq listing targeted ~Jul 1, 2026 (official filings say broadly H2-2026); BCA signed Feb 28 2026, Amendment No. 2 filed Jun 25 2026, F-4 filed May 26 2026 — F-4 effectiveness and shareholder-vote date not yet confirmed.
verified.instrument: equity (Class A shares; trust-floor downside ~$10.08/sh per Q1 10-Q)
verified.bloom_gates: G1 (named counterparty Pasqal Holding SAS) + G2 (concrete commitment: $2.0B pre-money BCA, $200M committed convertible, ≥$150M min cash at closing)
verified.edge: L — commodity de-SPAC arb on a soft close date; F-4 not yet effective and no fixed vote date, so the "Jul-1" milestone is a target not a hard catalyst. Upside is a pre-revenue quantum-hype re-rate (thin quality); the only hard floor is ~$10.08 trust.
verified.primary_source: SEC F-425 / 10-Q via StockTitan & Pasqal newsroom; BCA dated 2026-02-28, Amendment No.2 filed 2026-06-25, F-4 filed 2026-05-26 (verified 2026-06-29)
verified.analysis: Real, specific, dated, forward de-SPAC: Pasqal's definitive $2.0B combination with BBCQ (BCA 2/28/26, Amendment No.2 6/25/26, F-4 filed 5/26/26) targets a Nasdaq listing as "Pasqal" around early July 2026, with a ~$289.7M trust (~$10.08/sh) capping downside pre-vote. But the precise close date is soft — SEC filings frame timing as "H2 2026," the F-4 is not yet declared effective, and no shareholder-vote/redemption date is set, so the Jul-1 target is unconfirmed. The structure (trust floor + de-SPAC optionality on a pre-revenue, hype-driven quantum-hardware name) is a genuine catalyst but low-edge; warrants WATCH pending F-4 effectiveness and a fixed vote date, not active positioning.

verified.catalyst: Stacked, dated FDA catalysts: BBP-418 PDUFA Nov 27 2026 (potential first-ever LGMD therapy), encaleret NDA filed May 2026 (decision pending), and infigratinib achondroplasia NDA in 2H26 — atop a steep Attruby commercial ramp.
verified.dated_milestone: BBP-418 PDUFA target action date Nov 27, 2026 (NDA accepted, Priority Review, no AdCom planned); next: infigratinib NDA submission 2H26
verified.instrument: equity (BBIO common); event-vol favors long-dated calls/call spreads around the Nov 27 PDUFA
verified.bloom_gates: G1 (named counterparty FDA) + G2 (concrete commitment: accepted NDA with Priority Review and a fixed PDUFA date)
verified.edge: M — catalysts are real, dated and forward, but partly priced: FORTIFY hit all endpoints so BBP-418 approval is high-probability/well-telegraphed, stock at $71 near mid-range of $41.8–$84.94 52wk band, and the multi-asset GeneticDx narrative is well-followed. Edge is in the launch trajectory and the first-in-class LGMD label breadth, not surprise binary outcome.
verified.primary_source: BridgeBio PR via GlobeNewswire/StockTitan, 2026-05-27 (BBP-418 NDA acceptance, Priority Review, PDUFA Nov 27 2026) and Q1 2026 results 2026-05-07 (Attruby $180.6M; encaleret NDA filed May 12 2026; infigratinib NDA 2H26)
verified.kill_fact: All four load-bearing facts confirmed vs primary sources (BridgeBio IR/GlobeNewswire/SEC 8-Ks): BBP-418 NDA accepted w/ Priority Review, PDUFA Nov 27 2026, no AdCom, first-ever LGMD therapy, FORTIFY hit all endpoints (announced 2026-05-27); encaleret NDA submitted 2026-05-12 (CALIBRATE positive, launch early 2027); Attruby Q1'26 sales $180.6M (+24% q/q, +392% y/y), $500M buyback, total rev $194.5M. CORRECTION: line says infigratinib achondroplasia NDA 'in 2H26' but company guides Q3 2026 for the FDA NDA specifically (the 2H26 reference is the EMA MAA) — both land in 2H26 so directionally fine, slight date conflation. All three catalysts are forward/unfired. Edge is genuinely medium not high: positive Phase 3 reads de-risk/telegraph the approvals, name trades ~$14B cap mid-range, reward concentrated in launch slope not unpriced surprise — self-consistent with score framing.
verified.analysis: BBIO carries a genuine cluster of hard-dated, forward FDA catalysts: the BBP-418 NDA was accepted with Priority Review and a Nov 27, 2026 PDUFA (no advisory committee), encaleret's NDA was submitted May 12, 2026, and infigratinib's achondroplasia NDA is slated for 2H26 — all three Phase 3 programs hit their primary/secondary endpoints, so approval risk is reduced but the launches remain unfired. The base is already commercial: Attruby/acoramidis posted $180.6M Q1 2026 sales (+24% q/q, +392% y/y) toward ~$954M FY26 consensus, plus a $500M buyback. Edge is medium not high because the positive Phase 3 reads make BBP-418's binary largely de-risked/telegraphed and the name trades mid-52wk-range near a $14B cap, so reward is concentrated in launch slope and label breadth rather than an unpriced approval surprise.

verified.catalyst: Definitive Jan 11, 2026 SPAC merger with Exascale Labs (GPU-as-a-Service) to relist as XLAB; S-4/A on file, redemption-resistant ($5M min cash, 100% rollover).
verified.dated_milestone: Outside date Sept 1, 2026; S-4 not yet effective and no EGM/vote date set (proxy meeting fields still blank in S-4/A).
verified.bloom_gates: G1 (named counterparty Exascale Labs + definitive agreement/S-4); G2 partial (merger commitment dated but vote unscheduled). No G3 specific unpriced figure.
verified.primary_source: SEC S-4 (acc. 000182912626005168, filed 2026-05-14) and S-4/A (acc. 000182912626006917) for D. Boral ARC Acquisition I Corp.; FMP quote BCAR $10.39 (2026-06-29).
verified.analysis: The catalyst is real, named and dated (definitive Jan 11, 2026 agreement with Exascale Labs, $500M pre-money, S-4 filed May 14 + S-4/A amendments, ~$285M trust as of 12/31/25, redemption-resistant structure), and forward (not yet closed). But it is not the asymmetric merger-arb the triage implied: at $10.39 BCAR sits above its ~$10.16 trust/redemption value, so there is no near-trust downside floor to buy — the asymmetry is negative-to-thin, and post-close this is a momentum-driven, pre-scale GPU-as-a-Service relisting (effectively a thematic AI-infra bet) with going-concern language, not an unpriced value discount. No vote date is scheduled (meeting fields still placeholders) and the S-4 has not gone effective; the only hard date is the Sept 1, 2026 outside date. Real+dated+forward but fails strict edge gating, so WATCH at low conviction.

verified.catalyst: All-cash $70.00/sh acquisition by Aquarian Capital ($4.1B); definitive agreement signed and shareholder vote passed 99.7%, now pending insurance-regulatory approvals before close.
verified.dated_milestone: Shareholder vote PASSED Feb 12 2026; deal close expected in 2026 pending insurance-regulatory approvals (no firm dated close announced; next gate = state DOI / NYDFS clearances).
verified.bloom_gates: G1 (named counterparty: Aquarian Capital, $70/sh all-cash, $4.1B definitive merger) + G2 (concrete commitment: signed merger agreement + 99.7% shareholder approval Feb 12 2026)
verified.edge: M — ~10.3% gross spread to a signed, shareholder-approved all-cash deal is wide; width reflects genuine insurance-regulatory approval risk (PE acquirer of a large VA insurer; multi-state DOI/NYDFS scrutiny) and a soft "2026" close date, not a hard near-term catalyst, which caps the edge.
verified.primary_source: Businesswire/Brighthouse press release "Stockholders Approve Merger with Aquarian Capital" (Feb 12 2026) + Aquarian 11/6/2025 deal announcement ($70/sh, $4.1B); live price FMP quote BHF $63.46 (2026-06-29).
verified.kill_fact: All load-bearing facts confirmed vs primary sources: Aquarian Capital's $70.00/sh all-cash (~$4.1B) buyout of Brighthouse is a signed definitive merger agreement (Nov 6 2025; SEC 8-K + Businesswire), stockholders approved it at the Feb 12 2026 special meeting (BHF newsroom + Businesswire), and the deal remains pending with insurance-regulatory approval as the sole open condition (Seeking Alpha May 29 2026: 'expected to close in 2026 pending regulatory approvals'; active merger-referencing news through Jun 25 2026 = NOT fired). BHF trades $63.39 (FMP quote timestamp 2026-06-29) = ~10.4% gross spread to the $70 cash close. Forward, dated, specific merger-arb special-sit, not priced-out/re-rated. Minor unverified detail: the exact '99.7%' vote tally was not independently surfaced (the approval itself IS confirmed) and the spread/close-date framing ('2026' soft) is honest — wide spread reflects real regulatory tail-risk on a PE acquisition of a large VA insurer (state DOIs/NYDFS), correctly capping at 7 rather than a layup.
verified.analysis: Live, dated merger-arb special-sit verified: Aquarian Capital's $70.00/sh all-cash buyout of Brighthouse is a signed definitive deal, stockholders approved 99.7% on Feb 12 2026, and BHF trades $63.46 (FMP quote 2026-06-29) = ~10.3% gross spread to a 2026 close. The only open condition is insurance-regulatory approval (state DOIs / likely NYDFS), which is the source of both the spread and the risk — PE-backed acquisitions of large variable-annuity insurers can face slow, conditioned approvals. Real + specific + dated + forward (not fired/not re-rated), not a macro bet; capped at 7 because the close date is soft ("2026") and the wide spread signals real regulatory tail-risk rather than a layup.

verified.catalyst: No live activist catalyst: HoldCo's $35.8M position is a passive 13F holding (not a 13D), the only dated trigger (May 21, 2026 annual meeting) already passed with all 9 management nominees elected and no dissident slate, and no formal sale/strategic-review process exists.
verified.dated_milestone: No forward activist milestone; next dated event is Q2 2026 earnings on 2026-07-22 (not a catalyst for this thesis)
verified.bloom_gates: none — G1 no named counterparty/sale process, G2 no concrete company commitment to alternatives (management reaffirmed standalone), G3 no specific currently-unpriced figure
verified.edge: L — premise rests on a Jan 1, 2026 promotional "activist pressure" wrapper around a passive 13F; the cited proxy-battle trigger resolved as a non-event in May, and BKU is already +12.7% YTD (re-rated)
verified.primary_source: FMP stock news + WebSearch: SEC DEF 14A bkuproxy-20260410 & 8-K bku-20260521 (annual meeting 2026-05-21, all nominees elected); AOL/Investing.com (HoldCo 13F dated 2026-11-13 [prior yr], passive); MarketMinute/financialcontent.com promo dated 2026-01-01
verified.analysis: The triage's "proxy battle as 2026 annual meeting approaches" framing is stale: today is 2026-06-29 and the May 21, 2026 meeting already concluded with all nine management directors elected, no HoldCo dissident slate, and management reaffirming its standalone strategy. HoldCo's stake was disclosed via a 13F (passive), not a 13D activist filing, and there is no formal sale process or named acquirer; the "activist pressure" narrative came from a Jan-1 PR-wire promotional article, not a real campaign. With the only dated forward trigger now fired as a non-event and the stock already up 12.7% YTD, this fails the forward/unpriced/specific test — tier NONE.

verified.catalyst: No live catalyst — the $80/sh Clearlake take-private was a 2024 event (proposal Apr 15 2024, rejected by board May 16 2024) and is long dead, not "under board review."
verified.dated_milestone: None forward; all dated events are 2024 (poison-pill repeal 2024-03-18, $80 proposal 2024-04-15, board rejection 2024-05-16). Next scheduled item is bbcon conference 2026-09-29, not a deal.
verified.bloom_gates: None pass. G1 (named counterparty) and G3 (specific unpriced figure) were satisfied in 2024 but the catalyst is FIRED and re-rated against — Clearlake's $80 bid was rejected and abandoned over two years ago; no live G1/G2/G3 today.
verified.edge: L — the merger-arb thesis is falsified by the tape: BLKB trades $29.31 vs the alleged $80 offer (63% below), near its 52-wk low ($25.58), 200-DMA $49.94. A live take-private under review would pin the stock near $78; instead it collapsed after the deal died. No spread exists.
verified.primary_source: Blackbaud/PRNewswire "to Review Unsolicited Proposal from Clearlake" (2024-04-15) + Investing.com "Blackbaud rejects Clearlake's $80 buyout" (2024-05-16); current price FMP quote BLKB $29.31 (2026-06-29).
verified.analysis: The triage flag re-frames stale 2024 events as live: Clearlake (then ~18.9% holder) made an $80/sh unsolicited take-private proposal on 2024-04-15 after Blackbaud repealed its poison pill on 2024-03-18, and the board unanimously rejected it on 2024-05-16 as significantly undervaluing the company. Two-plus years later there is no pending deal — BLKB is independent at $29.31 (June 2026), down ~63% from the $80 reference and near 52-week lows, which is conclusive proof the bid is dead, not "hardening." This is a fired, re-rated catalyst with no forward dated milestone; fails the FORWARD and live-commitment gates.

verified.primary_source: Bloomin' Brands Q1 2026 earnings call transcript, May 7, 2026 (Motley Fool); Restaurant Business, Brazil refranchise close Dec 30, 2024; JANA settlement press release, eff. July 1, 2020.

verified.catalyst: FDA review of the completed tinlarebant NDA in Stargardt (STGD1) under Breakthrough Therapy Designation: 60-day filing-acceptance decision, then assignment of a PDUFA action date toward potential first-ever STGD1 approval.
verified.dated_milestone: FDA filing-acceptance decision ~60 days from June 12, 2026 completion = approximately mid-August 2026; PDUFA target action date assigned upon acceptance (not yet set).
verified.bloom_gates: G3 (partial): a concrete, currently-unpriced regulatory decision toward the first approved STGD1 therapy in ~53k US patients with zero approved competitors; the precise PDUFA date is not yet assigned. G1/G2 not met.
verified.edge: M — dated and genuinely forward (acceptance ~Aug 2026, PDUFA to follow), and STGD1 has no approved therapy, but the marquee de-risking binary (Phase 3 efficacy) already fired Dec 1, 2025 and the stock has re-rated (now $150 vs 52wk low $56), so the acceptance step is largely expected and the true approval binary still lacks a hard date.
verified.primary_source: Belite Bio GlobeNewswire press release, "Completes Rolling Submission of NDA... for Tinlarebant," June 13, 2026 (submission completed June 12, 2026); DRAGON Phase 3 topline GlobeNewswire release Dec 1, 2025.
verified.analysis: Verified: the rolling NDA was completed June 12, 2026 under Breakthrough Therapy Designation, triggering a ~60-day FDA filing-acceptance review (decision ~mid-August 2026), after which a PDUFA action date would be assigned — so a forward, dated regulatory binary genuinely exists and the triage framing is accurate. However the high-conviction catalyst (positive Phase 3 DRAGON topline, 36% lesion-growth reduction P=0.0033) already fired Dec 1, 2025 and the stock re-rated on it; the next step (acceptance under BTD) is incremental/expected, and the binary approval decision still has no hard PDUFA date. Real and dated but partially de-risked and not the marquee event, so WATCH rather than ACTIVE.

verified.catalyst: Bumble has engaged Morgan Stanley to explore a potential sale/take-private amid a collapsing user base, with Blackstone (~17-22% owner) and CEO/founder Wolfe Herd a possible sponsor for going private.
verified.dated_milestone: Reuters reported the Morgan Stanley engagement 2026-06-25/26; no formal process timeline or bid deadline disclosed — next checkpoint is Q2 2026 earnings (early Aug) and any 13D/A or 8-K confirming a process.
verified.bloom_gates: G1 partial (Morgan Stanley named as engaged advisor; Blackstone named major holder) — but NO named buyer/bidder, NO concrete commitment (G2), NO specific unpriced take-out figure (G3); premium expectations are modest (~20%) and partly already in the tape.
verified.primary_source: Reuters Exclusive "Bumble dating app explores sale, sources say" (2026-06-25), corroborated by GuruFocus/Globe and Mail (2026-06-26); FMP quote 2026-06-29 price $3.145, mkt cap ~$366M.
verified.analysis: BMBL is a genuine, dated, forward strategic-alternatives situation: Reuters (25-26 Jun 2026) reports Bumble engaged Morgan Stanley to weigh a sale/take-private, with Blackstone (~17-22%) and founder-CEO Wolfe Herd plausible go-private participants. However it is preliminary "exploration," not a signed/formal process — no named buyer, no committed terms, no concrete unpriced figure, and sources warn the company may simply stay independent. The catalyst is real and not yet fired, but it is only weakly gated (G1-partial: advisor named, not a counterparty) and the modest expected premium is already partly reflected after the +3.5% pop off the year-low, so edge is Low; this is a WATCH, not an ACTIVE high-conviction bet.
